content: So, what times would work for everyone? The idea would be to discuss how the rust-net branch and related would operate, and such.
If all of you are available for the weekly meeting (18:00 UTC) one of these weeks, that would be ideal. But I guess @FUJITA Tomonori may not be able to make it.
Sorry, I can't make it at 18:00 UTC (3:00 JST).
are there any other times suitable for everyone or should we do split meetings?
For the record, the times that won't work for me are (as far as I know) Tuesdays until 08:00 UTC, and Wednesdays 12:00~14:00 UTC, from mid-September now till mid-November

I recently attempted to write a net driver and found a lack of support for many APIs. The low-level interface parameters in C are based on pointers and there are too many changes involved. It is not convenient to handle pointers using Rust.So, has there been any discussion or planning regarding net-related support at this time?

@AllenX if you still need some guiding through the current practices we have around handling function pointers, I'd be happy to explain in a new topic. Generally, everything related to net support and how we've discussed it so far is in the Napi Abstractions topic
What about next week, Tuesday 10th, 13:00 UTC? i.e. 2023-10-10T13:00:00+00:00

Amélie Gonzalez said:

AllenX if you still need some guiding through the current practices we have around handling function pointers, I'd be happy to explain in a new topic. Generally, everything related to net support and how we've discussed it so far is in the Napi Abstractions topic

Thank you for sharing your experience in handling pointers, if convenient.
Is that meeting open? I'll attend if so
Sorry, I can't make it at 10th, 13:00 UTC.

Trevor Gross said:

Is that meeting open? I'll attend if so

Yeah, though if the key attendees that were interested on the networking side cannot make it cannot make it, we may need to reschedule. So far @Amélie Gonzalez and @Wedson Almeida Filho can join.
@FUJITA Tomonori @Davide Rovelli @Michele Dalle Rive It would be nice to know when (more or less) you could make it -- otherwise it is very hard to schedule.
11th, 12th and 13th, 13:00 UTC works for me. 17th, 13:00 UTC works too.
all dates proposed work for me
Thanks Tomonori & Davide.
Anytime works fine for me, thanks!
Were you going to invite someone like Andrew from the phy side, or is this just RFL?
Everybody is welcome -- I just invited Andrew in the list too.
Since Tomonori cannot make it today, let's do it one of the days he wrote above (I am waiting for Wedson's availability).

I'm not available from 13:00UTC to 14:30UTC on Wednesdays, but otherwise I should be good to reschedule whenever is more convenient. I'll just be mostly unavailable during the week of October 23rd (conference stuff and all), and at very specific moments, but you should rather try and find a time and date that works for those with the most restrictive set of constraints :smile:

AllenX said:

Amélie Gonzalez said:

AllenX if you still need some guiding through the current practices we have around handling function pointers, I'd be happy to explain in a new topic. Generally, everything related to net support and how we've discussed it so far is in the Napi Abstractions topic

Thank you for sharing your experience in handling pointers, if convenient.

Will do (and sorry for the delay), I will open a new topic shortly where I can dump some of the methodology I (and others, I learned by reading the original proposals of @FUJITA Tomonori and others after all) used for my driver

Miguel Ojeda said:

Since Tomonori cannot make it today, let's do it one of the days he wrote above (I am waiting for Wedson's availability).

I'm available on the 11th and 13th
For me either works. If somebody has a preference on one or the other, let us know. Let's try to do it tomorrow, and if that fails, the 13th?

That's gonna fail for me (11th is a Wednesday, I have a class at 13:00 UTC)
If the 11th is more convenient for everybody but me however, don't mind me
Ah, yes, sorry, my bad. Definitely, let's do it the 13th then. I will create the Google Meet.
If I forget anybody, please let me know and I will add you.

Andrew will try to join late on Friday (he told me he has a conflict).

In order to make it a bit easier for him, we could consider moving the one on Friday 30 minutes later or 1 hour. Would that be too late for you Tomonori? Would any of you have conflicts?
14:00 UTC? works for me.
@Miguel Ojeda did you already send an updated invitation for 14:00 UTC?
We will keep it as it is (we have extra attendees and I am not sure yet if Wedson could make it at the other time).
Agenda? I guess it depends on who attends.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Everybody is welcome -- I just invited Andrew in the list too.

May I attend the meeting as an observer?
